examples.title: Simple Machine Learning Classification
examples.description: Demonstrates basic supervised learning workflow for classification
examples.code:
// Simple ML Classification Workflow
CLASSIFIER SimpleML:
 DATA training_data[], labels[]
 MODEL trained_model
 
 FUNCTION train(training_data, labels):
 trained_model = initialize_model()
 REPEAT until convergence:
 predictions = model_predict(trained_model, training_data)
 error = calculate_loss(predictions, labels)
 model_update(trained_model, error)
 RETURN trained_model
 
 FUNCTION predict(trained_model, new_data):
 RETURN model_predict(trained_model, new_data)

// Usage
classifier = NEW SimpleML()
model = classifier.train(heart_disease_data, labels)
prediction = classifier.predict(model, patient_symptoms)
examples.explanation: Shows iterative training process where model learns by comparing predictions to actual labels and updating weights to minimize error

examples.title: Edge Computing Data Processing
examples.description: Demonstrates edge vs cloud decision logic for IoT data processing
examples.explanation: Shows intelligent routing of processing between edge and cloud based on latency requirements, data size, and device capabilities
examples.timeComplexity: O(1) for decision, O(n) for processing

examples.title: Federated Learning Workflow
examples.description: Demonstrates privacy-preserving machine learning across distributed devices
examples.code:
// Federated Learning Algorithm
CONST ROUNDS = 10
CONST CLIENTS = 100

FUNCTION federated_training(global_model, local_datasets):
 current_model = global_model
 
 FOR round FROM 1 TO ROUNDS:
 PRINT("Round " + round + "/" + ROUNDS)
 
 // Select subset of clients for this round
 selected_clients = random_select(local_datasets, 30)
 client_updates = []
 
 // Each client trains locally
 FOR EACH client IN selected_clients:
 local_model = copy(current_model)
 local_model = train_local(local_model, client.data)
 update = calculate_update(current_model, local_model)
 client_updates.append(update)
 
 // Aggregate updates (Federated Averaging)
 global_model = aggregate_updates(client_updates)
 current_model = global_model
 
 RETURN current_model

FUNCTION aggregate_updates(updates):
 // Weighted average of client updates
 total_weight = sum(update.weight FOR update IN updates)
 aggregated = zero_matrix()
 
 FOR EACH update IN updates:
 weight = update.weight / total_weight
 aggregated = aggregated + (update.parameters × weight)
 
 RETURN aggregated

// Key: Raw data never leaves client devices!
// Only model updates (gradients/weights) are shared
examples.explanation: Shows how federated learning trains models across distributed devices without sharing raw sensitive data, preserving privacy while benefiting from collective learning
